post_title: Raccoon Family Living in Columbus Chimney
post_body: We discovered a mother raccoon and her kits nesting inside the chimney flue. Installed a one-way door at the chimney cap and waited for the family to leave on their own before sealing the entry permanently.

post_title: Squirrel Chews Through Roof Vent in Brampton
post_body: A grey squirrel gnawed through a plastic roof vent to access the attic. We replaced the damaged vent with a steel-reinforced model and sealed all secondary entry points along the roofline.

post_title: Bat Colony Evicted from Milwaukee Church Attic
post_body: A colony of roughly 40 little brown bats was roosting in the attic of a historic church. We installed exclusion netting over all entry points and allowed the bats to leave naturally over several nights.

A homeowner reported strong skunk odour coming from beneath their back deck. We confirmed a striped skunk was denning underneath and installed a one-way exclusion door at the access point. Treated the area with professional odour neutralizer.

post_title: Mice Found Nesting in Denver Garage Insulation
post_body: Multiple house mice were discovered nesting inside the garage wall insulation. We sealed over a dozen entry points around the garage foundation and installed monitoring stations.

post_title: Raccoon Spotted in Windsor Backyard at Night
post_body: A large adult raccoon was repeatedly visiting a residential backyard and attempting to access the attic through a damaged soffit. We reinforced the soffit with galvanized steel mesh.

A groundhog burrowed under the fence and was eating vegetables daily. We installed an L-shaped exclusion barrier around the garden perimeter and sealed the burrow entrance after confirming the animal had vacated.

post_title: Bird Nest Blocking Hamilton Dryer Vent
post_body: A starling built a nest inside a residential dryer vent creating a serious fire hazard. We safely removed the nest after the fledglings departed and installed a bird-proof vent cover.

post_title: Chipmunk Tunneling Under Durham Region Porch
post_body: Multiple chipmunk burrows were found undermining a front porch foundation. We installed underground mesh barriers and sealed all visible entry holes with steel wool and caulk.

A homeowner woke to find a single bat flying in their bedroom. We identified the entry point at a gap in the attic soffit junction and installed a one-way valve allowing any remaining bats to exit safely.
